cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the options is wrong according to the news article provided. The article mentions that President Bush says, "...America's space program will go on," which directly contradicts option A. So A is incorrect. That might be the answer. Option B: "NASA hasn't found the cause of the Columbia disaster." The article states that NASA launched an investigation and said it was too early to nail down a precise cause. So B is correct as per the article. So B is not the wrong one. Option C: "Before the Columbia disaster, no shuttles had exploded in the course of landing." The article mentions that take-off and re-entry are the most dangerous parts and that in 42 years of US human space flight, there had never been an accident in descent to Earth or landing.
